records.m_pText: A Recent Genesis
I was thinking, [PCM1]…
What if this world had been created$na mere five minutes ago?
What?
Crystalist doctrine and scripture,$nhistoric temples, the rise and fall$nof nations…even our memories…
All that we believe has come to$npass may very well have been$ncreated a mere five minutes ago.
That thought inevitably creeps$ninto my mind when I see the$nGreat Chasm before my eyes.
…That would mean we were$ncreated five minutes ago, too.
Yet we can remember things$nbefore that.
Yes, but what if the world had been$ncreated five minutes ago along with our$nmemories of what has come to pass?
Created by who?
That I do not know,$nand yet…
When you wake in the morning, is your$nmind not full of events that were$nspontaneously created in your dreams?
So you’re saying that our memories of$nwhat transpired were created by$nsomeone a few minutes ago?
Yes, for can you say we truly$nexperienced the events in what$nwe believe to be the past?
I…I don’t know.
But even if the world were created$nfive minutes ago…
And even if our memories themselves$nwere created just then…
All I know is we have to close the$nGreat Chasm no matter what.
Yes, we must close the Great Chasm.
And to do that, we must$nawaken the crystals.
Shall we?
Yes, let us go.
The Two Journals
…I suspected as much.
Mm… This can’t be a coincidence,$n[PCM2].
You mean the journal that the$ndark knight Alternis Dim$ndropped?
Right. Except for some obvious$ndifferences in wear and tear,$nit looks exactly like that D’s$nJournal we have.
Right, but the similarities$ndon’t stop there.
The last half is quite different,$nbut the first half…it’s almost$nexactly the same.
It’s as if Alternis’s Journal$nsupplements the prophecy in D’s$nJournal.
Are…are they truly so similar?
That they are. Even the$nhandwriting and wording are$nalmost a perfect match.
So, [PCM2], you’re$nsaying D’s Journal is…is…
Well, we can safely assume$nAlternis’s Journal was written$nby Alternis Dim himself.
And the portion of D’s Journal$nhere that resembles it…
D’s Journal…$nWho…could have…$nArrrgh…!!
Are you alright,$n[PCM2]?
I’ll be fine…$nJust give me a little…$ntime to think.
The Two of You
Come to think of it, when I first$ncame to this land…
…you were the first one to$nspeak to me, [PCM2].
Was I?$nIt seems so long ago.
I do recall going over to talk$nto you after seeing you on the$nstreet corner.
Yes!$nThat was our very first meeting!
But the [PCM2] who I met$nwhen I first set foot on$nthis land…
…and the [PCM2] standing$nbefore me now…they somehow$nseem like two different people.
Two different people?
I don’t know what to say. But$nCaptain Owen did mention$nsomething.
What’s that?
He said things like, “Let’s go$nboar hunting again sometime,” or,$n“Where’ve you been these past$nfew days?”
But when I told him I had no idea$nwhat he was talking about,$nhe let out a hearty laugh and said$nI was quite the jester.
And then I met you at the Great$nChasm, [PCM1].
And after that, the black mage$nOminas Crowe put the city to$nflames and all four of us$nfinally met.
Hmm… My memory still fails me.
The only thing I can do now is to$ncompare D’s Journal to$nAlternis’s.
[PCM2]’s Sleep Talk
Hm?$nWhere’s [PCM2]?
He’s taking a little break.$nSaid something about not$nfeeling well.
Really? Didn’t he just say,$n“Leave the piloting to me”?
Is something the matter,$n[PCF2]?
Datz is looking for him.$nHe says the hull’s showing wear.
He needs some materials to make$nrepairs. I suppose I’ll just$nhave to wake him up!
Zzzzzz…$n*mumble mumble*
Hee hee, sleeping like a baby.$nWho knew he was so cute when$nhe sleeps?
…Black… Minus Strike…$nmastered…up to Black Bane.
…Unleash powerful combo…$nBut even with a thousand$nlives…
Stand ground… Auto-Phoenix…$nAutomatic HP recovery after$nbattle… Is there something$nelse? *mumble mumble*
…The grand marshal… Even that$nmove of his… Sure would be$nnice!
Enough of this gibberish,$nalready.
Wake up!$nDatz is looking for you!
Aw, what’s the harm in letting$nhim sleep a little longer?
Look… Doesn’t [PCM2] look$nas happy as a pig in the mud?
You’re right, he does. Ha ha.
[PCF1] vs. [PCF2]
Even if it’s you, [PCF2],$nsuch words are unacceptable!
Now, will you please take back$nwhat you said!?
Never! This is one matter in which$nI refuse to give an inch!
This is a clear-cut issue!$nBlack and white, with no$nroom for gray!
Wow, [PCF1] and [PCF2]$nare really going at it.$nDon’t see that too often.
I wonder what happened.$nAny ideas, [PCM1]?
*sigh*$nWhy don’t you ask them yourself?
Heh, I see you’re in fine form, too.$nWhat’s eating you?
How can you even say that’s the$nbest, [PCF1]?
I mean, your explanation doesn’t$nmake any sense!
And what of you, [PCF2]!?$nEverything is always black or$nwhite with you.
Never have I seen such$nobstinance!
I still don’t understand.$nWould you mind filling me in,$n[PCM1]?
I told you, ask them yourself.$n*sigh*
Baked custard with strawberry$nwhipped cream!
No! Sweet bean filled wafers!
Huh? Those are both desserts.$nAre you telling me this argument is$nabout which dessert is the best!?
That about sums it up.$n*munch munch*
Hm? What’re you eating there?
Strawberry whipped cream$ncustard with sweet bean filled$nwafers.
You make ’em yourself?
Yes, but it was really just a$nmatter of combining the two.$nRather tasty if I say so myself.
Rgh! You’re simply infuriating!
Me!? It is you who is utterly$nvexing!
I feel sorry for them…and you,$ntoo, [PCM1].
[PCM2]’s Tears
Look what we have here.$n[PCM2] must’ve dozed off.
Here? I wonder why.
Zzzzzz… *mumble mumble* I don’t$nmuch care for it, but with the$nspiritism job command…
Support abilities… Magic$ncritical, gloom, and$nmagic attack 10% up…
Adrenaline might also come in$nhandy…$n*mumble mumble*
Brave three times…$nThen, Fairy’s Aid followed by$nBlack Bane three times…
Brave once, then Rage followed$nby Minus Strike…$nI better practice some more.
Self-Restore would definitely$nbe a good move.$n*mumble mumble*
The Council of Six…combined$nstrength… Nothing to fear…$nWouldn’t you agree, Vic…?
Ha ha ha, what a bunch of gibberish.$nWake up, already, [PCM2]!$nYou’ll catch cold sleeping there!
Something the matter?
Perhaps we ought to let him$nsleep.
Look, [PCM1], [PCM2]$nis…$nHe’s crying.
What?
Zzzzzz…
Inevitability
This world, it’s…it’s different$nfrom the previous one,$nbut in subtle ways.
Yes, and it is not simply a matter$nof going back in time or a lapse$nin memory.
It’s the same world,$nand yet it’s different.
Events in this world are happening$njust a bit differently than$nwhat we’ve seen.
And the ladies here are$ndifferent, too.
But that suits me fine.$nKeeps things fresh and exciting.
A pity your personality hasn’t$nchanged, too.
This world does differ ever so$nslightly from the last one…
But why do you suppose the$nGreat Chasm is in the$nexact same place?
And why does Norende Village have$nto be swallowed up every time?
Now that you mention it,$nthat is strange.
I know… Maybe someone or something$nis out to get you, [PCM1].
Please, [PCF2], don’t make me$nout to be more than I am.
Perhaps Norende Village holds some$nsecret we have yet to fathom.
Who knows? But I have a feeling$nyou’re not far off the mark.
So the opening of the Great Chasm$nis inevitable…?
Unfathomable
The sage sure gave us a lot to$nthink about.
The dangers of the crystals and$nthe Harrowing…
The Harrowing…
To think he has studied it for$neighteen hundred years and is still$nin the dark about what it is…
I can’t help thinking that the$nHarrowing is what we’re going$nthrough right now.
You mean right here in this$nstrange world where time$nseems to have been rewound?
Do you think this strange world$nbears some relation to whatever$ncaused the Great Chasm?
Ah, well. Who can know?
Just like none can fathom the ways$nof a woman’s heart. At least that$npursuit, while just as pointless,$nis more enjoyable.
Who Has Changed?
…Now I understand!
Understand what, [PCF2]?
Holly and Barras must have run$ninto us a short while ago!
Huh? Us?
Ah, I see what you mean.$nThat would explain their reaction.
They met the versions of us$nfrom this world?
Umm…$nNow I am truly confused.
Come to think of it, I got a$ncompletely different impression$nof those two than the first$ntime we met them.
You mean their hearts weren’t$nblack?
I’d say it was more like they$nwere impossible to hate.
Maybe it was because I met them$nfor the first time, but I agree$nwith [PCF2]. They didn’t$nseem blackhearted.
Is that not because they are$ndifferent from those we met$nbefore?
Or perhaps, it is we who have$nchanged?
I wonder… But personally, I$ndon’t think I’ve changed.
……$nI agree. You haven’t changed$none bit, [PCM2].
But that’s something you can$nwork on.
The Sea Slug
The proprietress sure got angry.
Well, she is rather attached to$nthe ol’ Drunken Pig, you know.
Must’ve been a shock to see her$nbeloved tavern being run like$nthat.
What would possess anyone to$nname a tavern the Sea Slug?
Who knows? But it could be one$nof those places popular with$nthe local crowd.
The type with a cantankerous$nproprietor, die-hard patrons,$nand a stubborn refusal to serve$nstrangers.
No, I think not. The only clean$narea there was around the$nproprietor.
The knives, cutting boards, even$nthe barstools…they were all$ncovered in dust.
And the menu should have been$nfilled with the sea’s bounty, but$nall I could see were some dried$nnuts and dried meats.
You two sure have some sharp$neyes.
Hee hee, well, I guess I just$nnotice things that you don’t,$n[PCM1].
You mean food, right?
Ooh! What did you just say,$n[PCM2]!?
Shopping in Hartschild
Say, [PCF2], didn’t the$nproprietress ask us to pick$nsomething up when we went out?
Ah! I almost forgot the$nshopping list!
Datz and Zatz had some$nrequests, too.
This must be Datz’s list.$nLet’s see here…
“Coarsely ground sausage and$nHart salami, two barrels each.”$nAll that for one person?
And for Zatz, let’s see…$n“Cheese ‘with holes in it.’”
Why am I not surprised?
And the proprietress?$nWhat does she want?
“One barrel of salted pork, one$nclump of Eisen rock salt, and$nfive barrels of potatoes.”
Come to think of it, that’s an$nawful lot for us to carry all by$nourselves.
There’s more… “The gifts are for$nthe commander’s wife and any$ntownsfolk that help you.”
Gifts?
That’s right. She gave it to$nme when we left Grandship.
A barrel chock-full of dried$nseaweed and dried fish.
This town is ringed by volcanoes and$nplagued by civil war. I’m sure they$nwill be delighted to get some of$nthe sea’s bounty.
The proprietress sure thinks$nof everything.
How about we buy a gift for her$nthis time?
Great idea!
A Special Order
The friendship between the$ngrand marshal and swordmaster…$nWhat a heartwarming tale.
I had no idea. My father and$nSwordmaster Nobutsuna never$nspoke a word of it.
I don’t think they ever would.$nEven I can figure that much out.
Well, heartwarming tales are fine$nand all, but the rest of me$nis freezing.
[PCF2], what’s good to eat$nin this town?$nHopefully, it’s something piping hot.
At this hour, you’ll have to$nmake do with food carts.$nHow about that over there?
Pork miso stew and yuzu miso$nstew… Wait, is that “stew”$noden stew?
Well, the Eternian version probably$nisn’t what you’d expect.
We skewer daikon radish before$ncooking them in a special$nstock till they’re tender.
Then we dip them in pork or yuzu$nmiso sauces before eating them.
I have never heard of such a dish$nbefore. It warms my body$njust thinking about it!
Well, what’re we waiting for!$nI’ll have the pork miso stew!
Me, too!
I think I will try the yuzu miso stew.
Why am I not surprised?$nAnd you, [PCM2]?
Oh, me…?$nI’ll take it clean and spicy!
“Clean and spicy”?
Right, hold the sauce,$nadd mustard.
The stock is already so flavorful,$nit’s perfect with just that!
How could you possibly know that?$nYou haven’t eaten it before,$nhave you?
Nope, this will be my first—$nHmm, that is strange.$nHow did I know that?
Ha ha ha, you probably read it in$nthat journal or heard it from$none of your lady friends.
Come on, let’s hurry up and eat$nbefore it gets cold.$nLet’s eat, let’s eat!
Right…
[PCF2] Remembers!
Ah!$nI just remembered!$nI’ve been here before!
Really!? But, [PCF2],$nlast time you acted as if you’d$nnever seen this place before.
Well, heh heh…$nThat’s what I thought, but…
Have you remembered$nsomething?
Umm…how many years ago was that?$nI was still quite young.
I’d finished my lessons and was$nreturning to Eternian Central$nCommand from the city when I$ngot hopelessly lost.
Here in these passages?$nThat must’ve been awful.
It was. I walked and walked, but$nnothing I saw looked familiar.
I finally fell to my knees and$nbegan wailing like a baby.
That’s when my father found me.
He was running, and covered in$nsweat. “You had us worried to$ndeath,” he said.
A loving father desperately$nsearching for his dearest$ndaughter…
It was already late,$nso we settled down in a$nsmall cottage for the night.
Here in this village?$nIsn’t this where your mother and$nfather were born and raised?
That’s right. And the next day I$nreceived a good scolding.$nHeh heh…
He told me how he had Master$nNobutsuna and even Einheria$nout looking for me.
Ha ha ha. I imagine that was one$nepic scolding, alright.
But why are you remembering$nthis now?
That Eternian sweet bean bun$njogged my memory.
Eternian sweet bean bun?$nYou mean that pastry we had$nback in the city?
That enormous sugary monstrosity?
Yes, I wasn’t supposed to$nwaste my money on sweets,$nbut I bought one anyway.
It was so big, it cast a shadow$nover my head! Anyway, I was walking$nalong stuffing my face…
…when suddenly I realized I had$nstrayed far to the east of the$ncentral passage.
A ha ha ha!$nThat’s just like you, [PCF2]!
But I’m surprised the grand$nmarshal was able to find you.
About that… I found out later my$nfather had done something much$nthe same when he was a child.
Ha ha ha, like father,$nlike daughter!
That’s what Master Nobutsuna and$nEinheria said the following day.
Hee hee hee, your master and fellow$nstudent know you all too well.
That is just like you, [PCF2],$nto have food jog your memory$nlike that.
Heh heh…
Naval Attachment
No… I think we’ll repair the$nEschalot and keep on using her.
What’s going on?
Well, I was just talking to Datz$nand Zatz about the Eschalot.$nThey had an idea…
An idea? About the Eschalot?
That’s right! We’re using her as a$nsea vessel now, but as you know,$nthe Eschalot was originally an$nairship.
Right. The skystone that allowed$nher to fly was shattered.
Right, I’ve heard that story$nbefore.
But she’s still an airship.$nSure, she can sail the open sea,$nbut not as well as a real sea vessel.
Lucky for us, Grandship has plenty$nof seaworthy vessels stowed away.$nYou can take your pick.
If you want, we can get one of them$nready for you to use. That’s what$nwe were talking about.
Another ship? But I don’t have$nany problem with the Eschalot$njust the way she is.
And she’s fast enough for our$nneeds.
I agree. But above all, we have$nbecome quite attached to the$nEschalot.
Yes, it’s where I fought my very$nfirst battle, after all.
And even though I don’t recall ever$nsailing it, I took to the helm$nwith almost frightening ease.
We had a hard time at first when$nit attacked us, though.
True, but that led me to meeting$n[PCM1] and the others.
The Eschalot has been like$nanother member of our party$never since.
I suppose that settles it.
You folks have become attached to the$nold girl. And I don’t think anything$nI could say would change your mind.
You can say that again.
But we’re counting on you to$nkeep the Eschalot shipshape$nand seaworthy.
Course we will.$nJust leave it to us.
[PCM1]’s Dream
When I was young,$nI dreamed of becoming a sailor.
Is that so?
But [PCM1], weren’t you$nraised in the mountains?
That explains it right there.$nYou know, the grass is$nalways greener?
…In any case, I gave it up.
How come?
It’s hard to be a sailor if you$ncan’t swim, and I can’t.
Well, that is to be expected,$nconsidering you lived so$nfar from the sea.
Tsk, abandoning your dream for such$na trivial matter!$nWhat a shame!
I don’t think swimming is so trivial$nif you’re at sea all the time…
Listen, [PCM1],$nand listen good.
[PCM2] over there and all the$nduchy’s officers pilot airships!
But not one of them is able to$nfly on his own!
Straight-Laced [PCF1]
That young lady was amazing.
Such a carefree voice and super$ncute moves. And her songs made$nyou want to get up and dance.
Perhaps it is because of my long years$nat the temple, but I do not particularly$nfancy such noisy music…
Oh, you thought it was noisy$n[PCF1]?
Y-yes…$nIt makes my head go all fuzzy…
[PCF1], you simply must$nloosen up a bit!$nYou’re far too serious!
But is it not strange to surrender$nyour body to a beat and…prance$nabout like everyone else?
Strange? …Ha ha ha, no!$nIt’s fun to dance around without$na care in the world.
Besides, didn’t someone once say,$n“The most important thing is$nto have fun”?
You’ll miss out on what’s$nimportant if you’re always$nso serious.
Ah!$nI forgot something important!
I was so into her music, I forgot$nto go over and talk to her!
…Of course, in his case, it’s a$nmatter of having too much fun.
Reminds Me of Someone
Heinkel didn’t seem anything$nlike he was before, did he?
If only we could have spent more time$ntalking, and hearing each other out.
He reminds me of someone.
Who?
Someone with a strong sense of$njustice and quick to anger…
Ah…
…and who won’t budge once a$ndecision has been made.
Oh…
So, who is it?
Someone with whom you can’t have a$ncalm discussion about anything.
Are you going to tell us who it$nis or not!?
